body:
## Summary

- Replaces the `(*Config, []Warning, error)` and `([]Warning, error)` tuples returned by `orchestra.LoadConfig` / `orchestra.Validate` with a single `*ValidationResult` value carrying the parsed `*Config`, structured `Warnings`, structured `Errors`, and convenience methods (`Valid() bool`, `Err() error`).
- Hard break — `pkg/orchestra` is still Experimental, no shim. `Warning` grows a `Field []string` structured YAML path; new `ConfigError` type carries hard validation failures with the same shape. `ErrInvalidConfig` sentinel makes `errors.Is(err, orchestra.ErrInvalidConfig)` work.
- All five CLI call sites (`validate`, `run`, `plan`, `init`, `spawn`) and eight test files migrated. CLI byte-output is identical to pre-chapter — `Result.Err()` preserves the existing `"validation errors:\n  - ..."` format.

## Design doc

[docs/features/11-p25-validation-result.md](docs/features/11-p25-validation-result.md)

## Test plan

- [x] `make vet` — green
- [x] `make test` — green
- [ ] `go test -race ./...` — skipped locally (Windows host has no MinGW); CI runs this on Linux
- [x] `go tool golangci-lint run ./...` — 0 issues
- [ ] CI is the source of truth for the race build

## Notes

- Out-of-scope cleanup spotted but **not** done: [README.md:251](README.md) still references `orchestra.WithLogger(orchestra.NewCLILogger())`, which P2.4 removed. The design doc explicitly scoped the README change to line 241; flagging here for a P2.4 follow-up.

Generated with /ship-feature
title: P2.5: ValidationResult for LoadConfig / Validate
